content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Both amendments identify genuine data integrity issues in the plan_hash backfill logic. Amendment fr-2-e54db26f correctly identifies that snapshot_plan_hash() reads the current snapshot, which persist_plan() can advance independently (service.rs:370), causing legacy rows to be mislabeled with a newer plan version. Amendment fr-2-371a0969 correctly identifies an asymmetry: backfill_terminal_entry() (lines 2670-2689) only copies a caller-supplied hash with no snapshot fallback, while the non-terminal path (lines 3246-3249) does attempt snapshot backfill. Both are real correctness gaps that warrant tests and fixes.

## Votes

- `fr-2-e54db26f`: Accept
  Verified in source: lines 2916-2918 and 3246-3249 call snapshot_plan_hash() which reads the current snapshot plan_hash. persist_plan() at service.rs:370 sets snapshot.plan_hash to the hash of the latest plan JSON, advancing it on every plan update. A legacy row created under plan v1 that has plan_hash=None will be stamped with the current snapshot hash at replay time, which could be v2 or later. This is a silent data integrity bug — it misattributes work to the wrong plan version. The fix is sound: avoid snapshot-based backfill when the original plan version cannot be determined, and add a regression test covering legacy rows that span a plan evolution.
- `fr-2-371a0969`: Accept
  Verified in source: backfill_terminal_entry() (lines 2685-2689) only sets plan_hash when the caller provides Some(plan_hash). The terminal-replay path at lines 3218-3224 passes plan_hash directly from the caller. If a legacy finalized row has plan_hash=None and the caller also provides None (the common legacy case), the function will backfill run_id, outcome_detail, and finished_at but leave plan_hash as None. This is inconsistent with the non-terminal completion path at lines 3246-3249 which does attempt snapshot-based backfill. The requested regression test — preseeding a terminal row with plan_hash=None and replaying with caller plan_hash=None — is the correct way to document and guard against this asymmetry.
